english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Veritatem dico in Christo, non mentior: testimonium mihi perhibente conscientia mea in Spiritu Sancto:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλήθειαν λέγω ἐν χριστῷ οὐ ψεύδομαι συμμαρτυρούσης μοι τῆς συνειδήσεώς μου ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: I say the truth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam tristitia mihi magna est, et continuus dolor cordi meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι λύπη μοί ἐστιν μεγάλη καὶ ἀδιάλειπτος ὀδύνη τῇ καρδίᾳ μου
verses.king_james: That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Optabam enim ego ipse anathema esse a Christo pro fratribus meis, qui sunt cognati mei secundum carnem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ηὐχόμην γὰρ αὐτὸς ἐγὼ ἀνάθεμα εἶναι ἀπὸ τοῦ χριστοῦ ὑπὲρ τῶν ἀδελφῶν μου τῶν συγγενῶν μου κατὰ σάρκα
verses.king_james: For I could wish that myself were accursed from Christ for my brethren, my kinsmen according to the flesh:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui sunt Israëlitæ, quorum adoptio est filiorum, et gloria, et testamentum, et legislatio, et obsequium, et promissa:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινές εἰσιν ἰσραηλῖται ὧν ἡ υἱοθεσία καὶ ἡ δόξα καὶ αἱ διαθῆκαι καὶ ἡ νομοθεσία καὶ ἡ λατρεία καὶ αἱ ἐπαγγελίαι
verses.king_james: Who are Israelites; to whom *pertaineth* the adoption, and the glory, and the covenants, and the giving of the law, and the service *of God*, and the promises;

verses.latin_vulgate: quorum patres, et ex quibus est Christus secundum carnem, qui est super omnia Deus benedictus in sæcula. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν οἱ πατέρες καὶ ἐξ ὧν ὁ χριστὸς τὸ κατὰ σάρκα ὁ ὢν ἐπὶ πάντων θεὸς εὐλογητὸς εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Whose *are* the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ *came*, who is over all, God blessed for ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non autem quod exciderit verbum Dei. Non enim omnes qui ex Israël sunt, ii sunt Israëlitæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ οἷον δὲ ὅτι ἐκπέπτωκεν ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ οὐ γὰρ πάντες οἱ ἐξ ἰσραήλ οὗτοι ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: Not as though the word of God hath taken none effect. For they *are* not all Israel, which are of Israel:

verses.latin_vulgate: neque qui semen sunt Abrahæ, omnes filii: sed in Isaac vocabitur tibi semen:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδ᾽ ὅτι εἰσὶν σπέρμα ἀβραάμ πάντες τέκνα ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἰσαὰκ κληθήσεταί σοι σπέρμα
verses.king_james: Neither, because they are the seed of Abraham, *are they* all children: but, In Isaac shall thy seed be called.

verses.latin_vulgate: id est, non qui filii carnis, hi filii Dei: sed qui filii sunt promissionis, æstimantur in semine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τουτέστιν οὐ τὰ τέκνα τῆς σαρκὸς ταῦτα τέκνα τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλὰ τὰ τέκνα τῆς ἐπαγγελίας λογίζεται εἰς σπέρμα
verses.king_james: That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these *are* not the children of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Promissionis enim verbum hoc est: Secundum hoc tempus veniam: et erit Saræ filius.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπαγγελίας γὰρ ὁ λόγος οὗτος κατὰ τὸν καιρὸν τοῦτον ἐλεύσομαι καὶ ἔσται τῇ σάῤῥᾳ υἱός
verses.king_james: For this *is* the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall have a son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non solum autem illa: sed et Rebecca ex uno concubitu habens, Isaac patris nostri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ μόνον δέ ἀλλὰ καὶ ῥεβέκκα ἐξ ἑνὸς κοίτην ἔχουσα ἰσαὰκ τοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: And not only *this*; but when Rebecca also had conceived by one, *even* by our father Isaac;

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum enim nondum nati fuissent, aut aliquid boni egissent, aut mali (ut secundum electionem propositum Dei maneret),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μήπω γὰρ γεννηθέντων μηδὲ πραξάντων τι ἀγαθὸν ἢ κακόν, ἵνα ἡ κατ᾽ ἐκλογὴν τοῦ θεοῦ πρόθεσις μένῃ οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἀλλ᾽ ἐκ τοῦ καλοῦντος
verses.king_james: (For *the children* being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)

verses.latin_vulgate: non ex operibus, sed ex vocante dictum est ei quia major serviet minori,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐῤῥήθη αὐτῇ ὅτι ὁ μείζων δουλεύσει τῷ ἐλάσσονι
verses.king_james: It was said unto her, The elder shall serve the younger.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est: Jacob dilexi, Esau autem odio habui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται τὸν ἰακὼβ ἠγάπησα τὸν δὲ ἠσαῦ ἐμίσησα
verses.king_james: As it is written, Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo dicemus? numquid iniquitas apud Deum? Absit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν μὴ ἀδικία παρὰ τῷ θεῷ μὴ γένοιτο
verses.king_james: What shall we say then? *Is there* unrighteousness with God? God forbid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moysi enim dicit: Miserebor cujus misereor: et misericordiam præstabo cujus miserebor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ γὰρ μωσῇ λέγει ἐλεήσω ὃν ἂν ἐλεῶ καὶ οἰκτειρήσω ὃν ἂν οἰκτείρω
verses.king_james: For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur non volentis, neque currentis, sed miserentis est Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν οὐ τοῦ θέλοντος οὐδὲ τοῦ τρέχοντος ἀλλὰ τοῦ ἐλεοῦντος θεοῦ
verses.king_james: So then *it is* not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth mercy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit enim Scriptura Pharaoni: Quia in hoc ipsum excitavi te, ut ostendam in te virtutem meam: et ut annuntietur nomen meum in universa terra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει γὰρ ἡ γραφὴ τῷ φαραὼ ὅτι εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ἐξήγειρά σε ὅπως ἐνδείξωμαι ἐν σοὶ τὴν δύναμίν μου καὶ ὅπως διαγγελῇ τὸ ὄνομά μου ἐν πάσῃ τῇ γῇ
verses.king_james: For the scripture saith unto Pharaoh, Even for this same purpose have I raised thee up, that i might shew my power in thee, and that my name might be declared throughout all the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ergo cujus vult miseretur, et quem vult indurat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν ὃν θέλει ἐλεεῖ ὃν δὲ θέλει σκληρύνει
verses.king_james: Therefore hath he mercy on whom he will *have mercy*, and whom he will he hardeneth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicis itaque mihi: Quid adhuc queritur? voluntati enim ejus quis resistit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐρεῖς οὖν μοι τί ἔτι μέμφεται τῷ γὰρ βουλήματι αὐτοῦ τίς ἀνθέστηκεν
verses.king_james: Thou wilt say then unto me, Why doth he yet find fault? For who hath resisted his will?

verses.latin_vulgate: O homo, tu quis es, qui respondeas Deo? numquid dicit figmentum ei qui se finxit: Quid me fecisti sic?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μενοῦνγε ὦ ἄνθρωπε σὺ τίς εἶ ὁ ἀνταποκρινόμενος τῷ θεῷ μὴ ἐρεῖ τὸ πλάσμα τῷ πλάσαντι τί με ἐποίησας οὕτως
verses.king_james: Nay but, O man, who art thou that repliest against God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed *it*, Why hast thou made me thus?

verses.latin_vulgate: an non habet potestatem figulus luti ex eadem massa facere aliud quidem vas in honorem, aliud vero in contumeliam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ οὐκ ἔχει ἐξουσίαν ὁ κεραμεὺς τοῦ πηλοῦ ἐκ τοῦ αὐτοῦ φυράματος ποιῆσαι ὃ μὲν εἰς τιμὴν σκεῦος ὃ δὲ εἰς ἀτιμίαν
verses.king_james: Hath not the potter power over the clay, of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto dishonour?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si Deus volens ostendere iram, et notum facere potentiam suam, sustinuit in multa patientia vasa iræ, apta in interitum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ θέλων ὁ θεὸς ἐνδείξασθαι τὴν ὀργὴν καὶ γνωρίσαι τὸ δυνατὸν αὐτοῦ ἤνεγκεν ἐν πολλῇ μακροθυμίᾳ σκεύη ὀργῆς κατηρτισμένα εἰς ἀπώλειαν
verses.king_james: *What* if God, willing to shew *his* wrath, and to make his power known, endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to destruction:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut ostenderet divitias gloriæ suæ in vasa misericordiæ, quæ præparavit in gloriam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἵνα γνωρίσῃ τὸν πλοῦτον τῆς δόξης αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ σκεύη ἐλέους ἃ προητοίμασεν εἰς δόξαν
verses.king_james: And that he might make known the riches of his glory on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quos et vocavit nos non solum ex Judæis, sed etiam in gentibus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὓς καὶ ἐκάλεσεν ἡμᾶς οὐ μόνον ἐξ ἰουδαίων ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐξ ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: Even us, whom he hath called, not of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles?

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut in Osee dicit: Vocabo non plebem meam, plebem meam: et non dilectam, dilectam: et non misericordiam consecutam, misericordiam consecutam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς καὶ ἐν τῷ ὡσηὲ λέγει καλέσω τὸν οὐ λαόν μου λαόν μου καὶ τὴν οὐκ ἠγαπημένην ἠγαπημένην
verses.king_james: As he saith also in Osee, I will call them my people, which were not my people; and her beloved, which was not beloved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit: in loco, ubi dictum est eis: Non plebs mea vos: ibi vocabuntur filii Dei vivi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσται ἐν τῷ τόπῳ οὗ ἐῤῥήθη αὐτοῖς οὐ λαός μου ὑμεῖς ἐκεῖ κληθήσονται υἱοὶ θεοῦ ζῶντος
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, *that* in the place where it was said unto them, Ye *are* not my people; there shall they be called the children of the living God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Isaias autem clamat pro Israël: Si fuerit numerus filiorum Israël tamquam arena maris, reliquiæ salvæ fient.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠσαΐας δὲ κράζει ὑπὲρ τοῦ ἰσραήλ ἐὰν ᾖ ὁ ἀριθμὸς τῶν υἱῶν ἰσραὴλ ὡς ἡ ἄμμος τῆς θαλάσσης τὸ κατάλειμμα σωθήσεται
verses.king_james: Esaias also crieth concerning Israel, Though the number of the children of Israel be as the sand of the sea, a remnant shall be saved:

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbum enim consummans, et abbrevians in æquitate: quia verbum breviatum faciet Dominus super terram:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λόγον γὰρ συντελῶν καὶ συντέμνων ἐν δικαιοσυνῃ ὅτι λόγον συντετμημένον ποιήσει κύριος ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: For he will finish the work, and cut *it* short in righteousness: because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: et sicut prædixit Isaias: Nisi Dominus Sabaoth reliquisset nobis semen, sicut Sodoma facti essemus, et sicut Gomorrha similes fuissemus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθὼς προείρηκεν ἠσαΐας εἰ μὴ κύριος σαβαὼθ ἐγκατέλιπεν ἡμῖν σπέρμα ὡς σόδομα ἂν ἐγενήθημεν καὶ ὡς γόμοῤῥα ἂν ὡμοιώθημεν
verses.king_james: And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo dicemus? Quod gentes, quæ non sectabantur justitiam, apprehenderunt justitiam: justitiam autem, quæ ex fide est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν ὅτι ἔθνη τὰ μὴ διώκοντα δικαιοσύνην κατέλαβεν δικαιοσύνην δικαιοσύνην δὲ τὴν ἐκ πίστεως
verses.king_james: What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, which followed not after righteousness, have attained to righteousness, even the righteousness which is of faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Israël vero sectando legem justitiæ, in legem justitiæ non pervenit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰσραὴλ δὲ διώκων νόμον δικαιοσύνης εἰς νόμον δικαιοσύνης, οὐκ ἔφθασεν
verses.king_james: But Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare? Quia non ex fide, sed quasi ex operibus: offenderunt enim in lapidem offensionis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διατί ὅτι οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως, ἀλλ᾽ ὡς ἐξ ἔργων νόμου προσέκοψαν γὰρ τῷ λίθῳ τοῦ προσκόμματος,
verses.king_james: Wherefore? Because *they sought it* not by faith, but as it were by the works of the law. For they stumbled at that stumblingstone;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est: Ecce pono in Sion lapidem offensionis, et petram scandali: et omnis qui credit in eum, non confundetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ἰδού, τίθημι ἐν σιὼν λίθον προσκόμματος καὶ πέτραν σκανδάλου καὶ πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ οὐ καταισχυνθήσεται
verses.king_james: As it is written, Behold, I lay in Sion a stumblingstone and rock of offence: and whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.
